title: Agile / Scrum Policy
content: Sprint Planning is conducted at the beginning of each sprint. The development team selects backlog items based on priority and team capacity. The output of Sprint Planning is a Sprint Backlog.
content_preview: Sprint Planning is conducted at the beginning of each sprint. The development team selects backlog items based on priority and team capacity. The output of Sprint Planning is a Sprint Backlog.

title: Agile / Scrum Policy
content: Daily Scrum is a short meeting held every working day. The meeting should be time-boxed to 15 minutes. Each team member answers three questions: 1. What did I complete yesterday? 2. What will I work on today? 3. What obstacles are blocking my progress?
content_preview: Daily Scrum is a short meeting held every working day. The meeting should be time-boxed to 15 minutes. Each team member answers three questions: 1. What did I complete yesterday? 2. What will I work o

title: Agile / Scrum Policy
content: Sprint Review is conducted at the end of each sprint. The team demonstrates completed features to stakeholders. Stakeholders provide feedback which may lead to backlog updates.
content_preview: Sprint Review is conducted at the end of each sprint. The team demonstrates completed features to stakeholders. Stakeholders provide feedback which may lead to backlog updates.

title: Agile / Scrum Policy
content: A backlog item is considered done when: - Code is implemented - Code review is completed - Unit tests pass - Documentation is updated
content_preview: A backlog item is considered done when: - Code is implemented - Code review is completed - Unit tests pass - Documentation is updated

title: Change Management Policy
content: Any modification to project scope, requirements, or timeline must be submitted as a Change Request. The request must include a description of the change and justification.
content_preview: Any modification to project scope, requirements, or timeline must be submitted as a Change Request. The request must include a description of the change and justification.

title: Change Management Policy
content: The project manager and technical team evaluate the impact of the change. Impact analysis includes: - Schedule impact - Cost impact - Resource impact - Technical risks
content_preview: The project manager and technical team evaluate the impact of the change. Impact analysis includes: - Schedule impact - Cost impact - Resource impact - Technical risks

title: Change Management Policy
content: After analysis, the change request must be approved by the project manager or change control board (CCB). Only approved changes may be implemented.
content_preview: After analysis, the change request must be approved by the project manager or change control board (CCB). Only approved changes may be implemented.

title: Change Management Policy
content: Unauthorized changes should not be implemented. All changes must follow the formal change management process.
content_preview: Unauthorized changes should not be implemented. All changes must follow the formal change management process.

title: Software Quality Policy
content: All source code must undergo peer review before merging into the main branch. Code review ensures code quality, readability, and adherence to coding standards.
content_preview: All source code must undergo peer review before merging into the main branch. Code review ensures code quality, readability, and adherence to coding standards.

title: Software Quality Policy
content: Testing must include: - Unit testing - Integration testing - System testing Test cases should be documented and automated where possible.
content_preview: Testing must include: - Unit testing - Integration testing - System testing Test cases should be documented and automated where possible.

title: Software Quality Policy
content: Projects should use Continuous Integration (CI) pipelines. CI pipelines automatically run tests and checks whenever new code is pushed.
content_preview: Projects should use Continuous Integration (CI) pipelines. CI pipelines automatically run tests and checks whenever new code is pushed.

title: Software Quality Policy
content: Technical documentation must be maintained and updated regularly. Documentation includes architecture diagrams, API specifications, and user guides.
content_preview: Technical documentation must be maintained and updated regularly. Documentation includes architecture diagrams, API specifications, and user guides.

title: Risk Management Policy
content: Project teams must identify potential risks at the beginning of the project. Risks may include technical risks, schedule risks, or resource risks.
content_preview: Project teams must identify potential risks at the beginning of the project. Risks may include technical risks, schedule risks, or resource risks.

title: Risk Management Policy
content: Each risk should be evaluated based on: - Probability of occurrence - Potential impact on the project Risks should be categorized as low, medium, or high.
content_preview: Each risk should be evaluated based on: - Probability of occurrence - Potential impact on the project Risks should be categorized as low, medium, or high.

title: Risk Management Policy
content: For each high or medium risk, mitigation strategies must be defined. Mitigation may include additional testing, resource allocation, or schedule adjustments.
content_preview: For each high or medium risk, mitigation strategies must be defined. Mitigation may include additional testing, resource allocation, or schedule adjustments.

title: Risk Management Policy
content: Risks must be reviewed periodically during project meetings. New risks should be added to the risk register when identified.
content_preview: Risks must be reviewed periodically during project meetings. New risks should be added to the risk register when identified.
